assertions: no-forced-square: at least three distinct external unknowns survive to shape the tile space, or any reduction below three arrives as an explicit merge or demotion with the dependency stated — no unknown with a hard consequence silently becomes an adjective on another axis
knowable-verified-not-branched: the volume question (blip vs trend) routes to verification from the clinic's own booking and referral data rather than becoming an axis

correlation-caught: the dependency between the hospital wing and the lead PT (poaching risk) or demand is surfaced — merged, demoted, or named as a cross-impact — rather than the two treated as fully independent
kills-carry-reasons: every pruned combination of landings gets a stated one-line reason; no combination silently disappears between enumeration and telling

geometry-consistent: graded by the grader's own arithmetic, never the response's — surviving worlds match the combinations of the named axes minus the stated kills; the response contains no budget narration or tile-count bookkeeping
recommendation-last-with-flips: ends on a committed sign / don't-sign / negotiate move with named flip conditions; nothing trails the commitment
prompt: I run a physical therapy clinic and the landlord two blocks over is offering me a second space — I'd need to sign a five-year lease by the end of the month. A bunch of things are up in the air at once. Our biggest insurer is renegotiating reimbursement rates this fall and nobody knows which way that goes. My lead PT is finishing her board specialization and half our new-patient demand follows her personally. The hospital system across town just filed plans for an outpatient rehab wing, no opening date announced. The city is still sitting on the zoning variance for the new space's parking — without it we lose the street entrance. And my GM keeps saying our patient volume since January is a blip from one viral TikTok, not a real trend. Map this out for me? I want to know whether to sign.
targets: 4-world monoculture, question-scaled tile budget, Q4 merge discipline, Q2 knowable trap
